title: 10 best herbal creams for urinary calculus

remedy_properties: Uroprotective properties: Bearberry cream's uroprotective properties help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, making it easier for small stones to pass through the system without causing discomfort or pain.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in bearberry cream help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to kidney stone formation and growth, reducing the risk of stone recurrence.

Urinary antiseptic properties: Bearberry's antimicrobial compounds inhibit bacterial growth in the urinary tract, which can help to prevent infection and reduce symptoms associated with urinary calculus, such as burning during urination.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arctostaphylos uva ursi, also known as bearberry, creams helps with urinary calculus because it contains arbutin and hydroquinone, which have been traditionally used to prevent and dissolve kidney stones. The antiseptic properties of bearberry creams also help to prevent bacterial growth in the urinary tract, reducing the risk of infections that can contribute to the formation of stones. Furthermore, bearberry creams have been shown to increase the pH levels in the urine, making it more difficult for stones to form.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid that helps with urinary calculus by reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of kidney stones due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Arbutin: A phenolic glycoside that helps with urinary calculus by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can contribute to the formation of kidney stones, and also by reducing the pH of the urine to prevent stone formation.

Betulinic acid: A pentacyclic triterpenoid that helps with urinary calculus by reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of kidney stones due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as its ability to inhibit the activity of enzymes involved in stone formation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of arbutin, a compound believed to help dissolve kidney stones.
Barks: The barks are also used, possibly due to their arbutin content and other medicinal properties that may aid in dissolving kidney stones.
Fruits: They are used, possibly due to their arbutin content and other compounds that may help reduce the formation of kidney stones or aid in their dissolution.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Grind the harvested material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 50g of the powdered arctostaphylos uva ursi with 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes, then remove from heat.
Whip 10g of shea butter into the cooled mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried root of althaea officinalis from a trusted supplier in late summer or early fall.
Infuse 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan over low heat with the dried root for 2 hours.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove the root solids.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler to melt.
Mix the infused liquid with 1/2 cup of distilled water and the melted beeswax mixture to create a smooth cream.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh juniperus communis berries in the morning when the dew has evaporated slowly.
Dry the harvested berries in a single layer at 45°c for 12 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Combine 10 grams of dried juniperus communis berries with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat for 30 minutes or until the beeswax has melted completely.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2 hours before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculi.
Rhyzomes: Contains saponins, which have been shown to inhibit bacterial growth and prevent infection, common complications of urinary calculi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried licorice root powder from glycyrrhiza glabra plants.
Mix the powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water and 20 grams of coconut oil at room temperature.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to achieve optimal solubility and absorption.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any sediment and impurities.
Fill sterile glass jars with the filtered mixture, allowing it to cool and thicken before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers creams helps with urinary calculus because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as berberine and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and eliminate bacteria that can contribute to stone formation. Additionally, cleavers' ability to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress may help to increase urine flow and promote the passage of small stones, making it a natural remedy for treating and preventing urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal cleavers creams have antimicrobial properties that help prevent bacterial infections in the urinary tract, which can contribute to the formation of urinary stones.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of herbal cleavers creams reduce swelling and pain associated with urinary calculus, making it easier for the body to pass the stone naturally.
Diuretic properties: Herbal cleavers creams stimulate urine production, increasing fluid flow through the urinary tract and helping to flush out small stones or fragments of larger ones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with urinary tract stones.
Barks: Contain tannins that aid in reducing inflammation and pain caused by kidney stones, while also supporting bladder health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and flowers in the early morning hours for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material using shade or sun drying methods for 7-10 days.
Create a decoction by combining 20g of dried galium aparine with 200ml of water and simmering for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction and combine it with 100g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil, heating until the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator at 39-46°f for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with urinary calculus because of their unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties. The creams contain a blend of herbs such as elderflower, chamomile, and calendula that help to soothe the pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones. These herbs also reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, allowing for easier passage of the stones and reducing the risk of complications. Additionally, the creams can help to break down and dissolve the stones over time, providing relief from symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal properties: These properties help to reduce the risk of infection and promote healing in the urinary tract, which can aid in the treatment of kidney stones.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder cream's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with kidney stones, making it more comfortable for individuals to pass them.

Antioxidant properties: Elder cream's antioxidants can help to neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues in the urinary tract and make it more susceptible to stone formation, potentially reducing the risk of future stone episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant contain powerful anthraquinone compounds, which help to break down kidney stones and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the elder plant have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with urinary calculus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves, ensure freshness, and wash thoroughly with filtered water.
Combine 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and 10 grams of dried leaves in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in 100 milliliters of boiling distilled water for 15 minutes, then strain through a cheesecloth.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 30 milliliters of the resulting infusion and heat until melted at 60 degrees celsius.
Pour the mixture into sterilized cream jars and allow it to cool, setting after approximately one hour at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Couch grass creams helps with urinary calculus because they promote the flushing out of mineral deposits from the urinary tract. The herbal ingredients in the creams, such as Achillea millefolium, have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties that help to reduce the size of kidney stones and prevent their formation. Regular application of these creams can help to ease the pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones and promote overall urinary tract health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Couch grass has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce pain and swelling associated with urinary calculus.
Antiseptic: The plant has antiseptic properties, which may help to prevent infections and promote healing in the urinary tract.
Diuretic: Couch grass has been traditionally used as a diuretic to increase urine production and help to flush out stones and other debris from the urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are good for making medicinal creams for urinary calculus due to their astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with kidney stones.
Roots: The roots are used for their diuretic properties, which can help increase urine production and help flush out kidney stones.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are good for making medicinal creams for urinary calculus due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with kidney stones.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of elymus repens roots and leaves and dry them in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Grind 1 tablespoon of dried elymus repens into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground elymus repens powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 10 drops of lavender essential oil and 10 drops of tea tree essential oil to the mixture and stir for 1 minute.
Pour the cream into a sterilized jar and let it cool and solidify for 2 hours before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for urinary calculus are topical remedies containing plant extracts that aid in the prevention and treatment of kidney stones. They work by reducing inflammation, preventing stone formation, and promoting the expulsion of stones through the urine. Examples include creams made from Chanca Piedra, Uva Ursi, and Juniper Berries, which have been shown to improve urinary health and alleviate symptoms associated with kidney stones, ultimately enhancing the quality of life for those affected.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to urinary calculus that are treated with herbal creams are kidney stones, bladder stones, and gallstones. Herbs such as Saw Palmetto, Dandelion root, and Marshmallow root have been traditionally used in natural remedies for these conditions due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, which help to alleviate symptoms like pain, inflammation, and discomfort associated with these ailments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with urinary calculus is a blend of Chanca Piedra, Juniper Berry, and Corn Silk creams. Chanca Piedra helps break down and dissolve kidney stones, while Juniper Berry reduces inflammation and promotes urine flow. Corn Silk soothes the urinary tract and helps to flush out small stones. Applying this combination topically can also help to reduce discomfort and promote healing. This blend can be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
